summary: Tested Emergent Google Auth integration end-to-end (backend + frontend). 17/17 backend pytest cases pass (auth gating, public reads, mutations 401/403/200, history source=manual, logout, session validation). Frontend: visitor sees login-btn + 'lecture seule' tag, 0 mutation buttons, no HAROPA refresh button. sheet-toggle correctly switches .sheet class between 'open' and 'closed'. Admin via injected localStorage token sees logout-btn + refresh-haropa-btn + 13+ set-ouvert-* buttons; clicking set-ferme-pont-7 updates badge live to 'Fermé'; logout returns to visitor mode.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/auth/session sets HttpOnly cookie AND returns session_token in body; frontend stores body token in localStorage. Cookie is unused in the K8s ingress flow (CORS=*). Could be removed for clarity but not blocking.

haropa_loop creates extra works with id 'haropa-<id>' when names don't match the seed map. After a sync, /api/works returns more than 13 items (saw 16 during testing). Earlier test 'test_list_works_returns_13' (now removed) would have become flaky — current test asserts >=13.
get_current_user returns None on expired sessions but does not delete them; stale rows accumulate in user_sessions. Add a TTL index on expires_at if cleanup matters.
App.js: setStatus failure path only updates subLabel; no toast/visible error to user. Minor UX improvement.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_portpasslh.py (deleted - obsolete; new file replaces it with auth-aware tests)
test_credentials: Admin allowlist email: mrxxdoxdoxx@gmail.com. Sessions seeded directly in MongoDB (collections users + user_sessions) since Emergent Google flow is not automatable. Tokens prefixed TEST_ are cleaned up by fixtures.
seed_data_creation: Per-test admin/non-admin user+session inserted via mongosh and removed in fixture teardown. One persistent FE admin session token (TEST_FE_1778003987550) was injected into localStorage for the Playwright admin run; can be cleaned with db.user_sessions.deleteOne({session_token:'TEST_FE_1778003987550'}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_portpasslh_auth.py as the regression file; it self-seeds admin & user sessions through mongosh. /app/memory/test_credentials.md documents the manual mongosh recipe for injecting admin sessions when bypassing Google OAuth. Frontend admin can be simulated by writing a valid session_token to localStorage key 'ppl_session_token' then reloading.
